titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 610

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=916 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 16:22:02.007249+00 | translation moa=917 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 16:22:02.018827+00

p.cl: W Rex Scott’ . Iusticiis suis . et vicecomiti suo de Berewic . Salutem .

prohibeo firmiter
ne seuienties mei uicecomitatus de Berewic . alias habeant consuetudines . aut alias
faciant exacciones . in elemosina mea de Goldingham . et Goldinghamshire de
placitis . et querelis quas ipsi mouent  quam seruientes mei aliorum uicecomitatuum
meorum habent in aliis elemosinas meis in terra mea .

Testibus Eng’ Episcopo de Glasg’
Nich’ Cancellario . Dauid . Olif’ Iustic’ . Hug’ Rid’ . Rob’ Auenel .
William king of the Scots to his justices and sheriff of Berwick greeting.

I strictly forbid that my sergeands of the sheriffdom of Berwick may have any customs or make any demands in my alms of Coldingham and Coldinghamshire in respect of pleas and plaints which they move, which my sergeands of my other sheriffdoms have in my other alms in my land.
As witnesses, Ingram bishop of Glasgow, Nicholas the chancellor, David Oliphant justice, Hugh Ridel, Robert Avenel.
